This family consists of approximately 66 genera and over 1,800 species of flowering plants. Aristea wredowia is an endemic species found in the southwestern region of the Western Cape province in South Africa.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
